mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress corporate earnings expectations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth that sustains corporate revenue trajectories, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when retail sales growth exceeds 3% but housing starts decline, or Rank IC remains negative over 5-year out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during periods of stable inflation signals robust supply-side capacity utilization that supports near-term equity price appreciation over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in >70% of observations during low-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings exceeding a 0.4% threshold trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and wage income that directly supports consumer spending and corporate profit growth over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth correlates with negative 60-day forward returns in >55% of observations over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP expansion and monthly retail sales growth confirm broad-based demand strength that reduces recession probability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in >65% of quarters where both series show positive growth.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% indicates immediate supply chain disruptions and reduced factory output that force equity markets to price in lower near-term earnings.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production shocks are followed by positive 5-day forward returns in >60% of triggered instances.
